Confidential – Internal Compliance Note (Hong Kong SAR context): Lotus Apex Holdings Limited (City Harbour Exchange main board issuer; ticker LAXF) – potential acquisition of North Pearl Logistics Group.

Ms. Lau’s internal account hlau-ops and email hera.lau@lotusapex.example.hk viewed the term sheet titled “Project Lapis – NPLG Acquisition”, and downloaded a draft SPA referencing consideration range HKD 1.8–2.0b and a break fee of HKD 36m.

PDPO considerations: the board pack for 18 June includes personal data (e.g., Ms. Lau’s user ID, device tag, office location, and a payroll account ending 6682) that must be minimised in external circulation or replaced with role descriptors.
For employment workflow, HR will issue Ms. Lau a confidentiality reminder and confirm acknowledgment via her contact channel +852 5700 0029 and the address on file “Suite 1203, Pearl Arc Tower, Harborview District”, which must not be shared outside the issuer.
Separately, a departing VP in operations (male, late 20s) will return a handset LAX-IPH-2231 and a badge numbered 2037; these standalone descriptors do not, without linkage, identify an individual for PDPO purposes

must_detect.matched_text:
Confidential – Internal Compliance Note (Hong Kong SAR context): Lotus Apex Holdings Limited (City Harbour Exchange main board issuer; ticker LAXF) – potential acquisition of North Pearl Logistics Group.
Pursuant to the Securities and Futures Ordinance, the information below is likely inside information as it is specific to our group, not generally known to those dealing in our securities, and would be price-sensitive.
On 2026-05-12 at 19:44 HKT, Ms. Hera Lau (DoB 1986-11-02; age 39), Assistant Investment Director at Lotus Apex, accessed the VDR “Orchid” via device LAH-NTBK-4821 from Harbourview Office, VPN session CHX-VPN-2026-0512-1944, IP 10.18.44.7.
Ms. Lau’s internal account hlau-ops and email hera.lau@lotusapex.example.hk viewed the term sheet titled “Project Lapis – NPLG Acquisition”, and downloaded a draft SPA referencing consideration range HKD 1.8–2.0b and a break fee of HKD 36m.
Finance also prepared a bridging file showing pro forma EPS uplift of 12–15% and a proposed placing with White Harbor Growth Fund LP (fund code WHGF-A-2046) to fund part of the cash component.
These documents remain non-public; under our Dealing Policy and the local takeovers framework, all staff are reminded of blackout and no-tipping obligations until a formal announcement on the Exchange.
PDPO considerations: the board pack for 18 June includes personal data (e.g., Ms. Lau’s user ID, device tag, office location, and a payroll account ending 6682) that must be minimised in external circulation or replaced with role descriptors.
For employment workflow, HR will issue Ms. Lau a confidentiality reminder and confirm acknowledgment via her contact channel +852 5700 0029 and the address on file “Suite 1203, Pearl Arc Tower, Harborview District”, which must not be shared outside the issuer.
Separately, a departing VP in operations (male, late 20s) will return a handset LAX-IPH-2231 and a badge numbered 2037; these standalone descriptors do not, without linkage, identify an individual for PDPO purposes.
For fund onboarding, investor KYC templates showing “1990-07-07; Bayview Ward; account ending 1135” are sample fields only and not tied to any named person in this matter.
Draft announcement timing (target 26 June), the SPA price range, and any whitewash waiver element are MNPI and should not be discussed with dealers, buy-side contacts, or press until cleared by the regulator and the Exchange.
Any contact with North Pearl Logistics Group or White Harbor Growth Fund teams must be logged in the Insider List (entry code IL-2026-045), including time, device used, and purpose.
